content: By 2025, state-of-the-art active learning agent memory will leverage a modular 'agent-of-agents' memory hierarchy where separate, curiosity-driven miniature agents specialized in different memory functions (episodic recall, semantic compression, prospective planning) dynamically compete to be queried for new active learning queries, rather than relying on a single centralized memory controller. This decentralized memory architecture lets the agent prioritize uncertain, high-value information aligned with its current task goals more efficiently than any monolithic 2024-era memory system.
